name: baseline_2026-05-08
_comment: Baseline Reference Case - Conservative assumptions for IRP planning

_parameters.heating_factor: Space-heating-only calibrated therms-per-HDD-per-unit for the current scope
_parameters.vintage_heating_multipliers: Multiplier on heating_factor by building vintage era. Older homes use more heat.
_parameters.segment_heating_multipliers: Multiplier on heating_factor by segment. MF uses less (shared walls).
_parameters.repair_efficiency_recovery: Efficiency recovery when old equipment is repaired (85% of original)
_parameters.new_equipment_efficiency: AFUE of replacement equipment (92% code minimum)
_parameters.new_construction_mf_share: Share of new construction that is multi-family (37%)
_parameters.hybrid_adoption_rate: Share of replacements that go to dual-fuel heat pump + gas backup (3%/yr)
_parameters.hybrid_gas_usage_factor: Gas usage of hybrid homes relative to gas-only (35% — heat pump handles mild weather)
_parameters.use_recs_ratios: Use RECS 1993-2020 trend to compute non-heating end-use ratios dynamically
_parameters.use_census_enrichment: Enrich unclassified premises with Census B25024/B25034 distributions
_parameters.baseline_irp_alignment: Anchor this baseline scenario estimated total UPC to NW Natural IRP UPC when the IRP forecast is available.
